mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment rate rises above 7.0%, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE > 7.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 836k units indicates moderate housing activity
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining below 98.5 signals supply-side weakness that constrains profit margins and earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns when INDPRO < 98.5 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating below 50k new jobs per month signals labor market momentum loss that reduces consumer income and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136,038k indicates large absolute employment base
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when PAYEMS growth < 50k are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2.0%) signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of co-triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) expanding above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 15,648.7B indicates solid economic base
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during periods of GDPC1 growth > 2.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) both declining signals broad-based economic weakness that reduces corporate capacity utilization and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline month-over-month simultaneously
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 5-year window.
